personalInfo.name: Sonam Kumari
personalInfo.title: Frontend Engineer
personalInfo.headline: I build scalable frontend systems, not just interfaces.
personalInfo.intro: Frontend Engineer with 3+ years of experience building scalable UI systems and production-grade React applications. Specialized in design systems, micro-frontends, and performance optimization.
personalInfo.jobStatus: Currently leading frontend development at Cygeniq with end-to-end ownership from requirement gathering to Figma design and implementation.

highlights: Owned complete frontend development lifecycle (requirements → design → implementation)
Built reusable component library improving Core Web Vitals performance by 60%
Implemented scalable theming system using design tokens across multiple apps
Integrated Playwright for end-to-end testing coverage
Collaborated with backend teams for API design and system integration
Implemented micro-frontend architecture using Webpack Module Federation
Improved load performance by 30% through bundle optimization strategies
Refactored state management from legacy Redux to Redux Toolkit
Built fully accessible UI components with ARIA standards compliance
Optimized bundle size reducing initial load by ~30%

caseStudies.title: HexaShield
caseStudies.description: Took over the frontend development of an existing security product (Version 1), driving its evolution from requirement gathering to Figma design and new feature deployments.
caseStudies.problem: An existing complex security product needed scalable new features, UI improvements, and architectural refinements to handle rapidly evolving requirements.
approach: Translated new business requirements into detailed UI flows and wireframes
Designed interfaces in Figma to incrementally improve the existing application
Refactored and expanded the existing React codebase with reusable components
Integrated complex REST APIs maintaining robust error handling
impact: Successfully scaled the application from Version 1 to a more robust platform
Improved usability scores and feature consistency across the evolving modules
Established a scalable component architecture for all future development

caseStudies.title: UX Common Components
caseStudies.description: Architected and built a scalable, reusable component library to standardize UI across multiple enterprise applications.
caseStudies.problem: Multiple applications had inconsistent UI patterns, duplicated component code, and poor performance across the product suite.
approach: Audited existing components across products and identified duplication
Created a unified component architecture with strict API contracts
Implemented a theming system with light/dark mode via CSS custom properties
Documented all components with usage guidelines for developers
impact: Improved Core Web Vitals performance by ~60% across consuming apps
Eliminated redundant development effort saving ~40% of UI dev time
Enhanced developer experience with a consistent, well-documented API

caseStudies.title: Micro-Frontend Architecture
caseStudies.description: Designed and implemented a micro-frontend architecture enabling independent team deployments and eliminating release bottlenecks.
caseStudies.problem: A monolithic React application was causing deployment bottlenecks, slow CI/CD cycles, and was impossible for multiple teams to work on simultaneously.
approach: Evaluated Module Federation as the primary micro-frontend strategy
Separated features into independently deployable applications
Designed shared dependency management to avoid version conflicts
Implemented optimized per-app lazy loading and bundling strategies
impact: Enabled independent deployments reducing release cycle by 60%
Improved scalability enabling 3 teams to ship independently
Reduced initial bundle size by 30% through strategic code splitting

seo.title: Sonam Kumari | Frontend Engineer
seo.description: Frontend Engineer specializing in React, TypeScript, micro-frontends, and scalable UI systems. 3+ years building production-grade applications.
